events.title: Food Truck Friday at Garver Feed Mill
events.description: 1. Hello everyone! Please join me for another fun, casual evening out, this time at Garver Feed Mill in the Atwood Neighborhood. This place is so much fun with ample seating, a great selection of food and live music. Look for me where the band is, hopefully I’ll be able to score the comfy sofa area. If not, I’ll try and get us a table in the same area. This is a rain or shine event. If there is bad weather we can play games at Roll Play, the Korean restaurant inside. Hope to see you there!

events.title: After Hours Olbrich Botanical Gardens
events.description: Hello ladies! This is my first event as substitute organizer and I hope you can join me at the Olbrich Botanical Gardens. During the summer, they have a special after hours events every Tuesday for the public, you do not have to be a member to attend. You still need a ticket, they are free, click on this link to get one: https://www.olbrich.org/calendar/after-hours. Please join me, have a glass of wine and enjoy live music in this beautiful setting!

events.title: Pontoon boat ride
events.description: The Madison School and Community Recreation (MSCR) offers drop in Pontoon boat rides at various locations during the summer months. On occasion, Michelle from our group and her husband operate these boats on a volunteer basis. Rides cost $5 and you pay in person on location when you register. Boat capacity is only 15 people and tickets are not available in advance. Actual ride is 90 minutes and starts at 1:30 pm. Ticket sales start 45 minutes ahead of time and I will be there at 12:30 pm. Book Discussion: Before the Coffee Gets Cold by Toshikazu Kawaguchi
events.description:
**Before the Coffee Gets Cold by Toshikazu Kawaguchi**

**We're going to try something new for this discussion:** we'll meet at Lakeside St Coffee House, then walk along the lake as we talk!

\*\*\*What would you change if you could go back in time? \*\*\*

In a small back alley in Tokyo, there is a café which has been serving carefully brewed coffee for more than one hundred years. But this coffee shop offers its customers a unique experience: the chance to travel back in time.

In *Before t...

events.title: Book Discussion: There is No Antimemetics Division by qntm
events.description:
**There is No Antimemetics Division by qntm**

**Humanity is under assault by malevolent "antimemes"—ideas that attack memory, identity, and the fabric of reality itself—in this wickedly brainbending tale of science-fiction horror, an entirely reimagined and expanded version of the beloved online novel.**

They’re all around us, hiding in plain sight.

One could be in the room with you, now, just to your left. You could be *seeing* it right now—but from this second to the next, you’ll forget ...

events.title: Book Discussion: Automatic Noodle by Annalee Newitz
events.description:
**Automatic Noodle by Annalee Newitz**

**From sci-fi visionary and acclaimed author Annalee Newitz comes Automatic Noodle, a cozy near-future novella about a crew of abandoned food service bots opening their very own restaurant.**

While San Francisco rebuilds from the chaos of war, a group of food service bots in an abandoned ghost kitchen take over their own delivery app account. They rebrand as a neighborhood lunch spot and start producing some of the tastiest hand-pulled noodles in the c...

events.title: Book Discussion: Ancillary Justice by Ann Leckie
events.description:
**Ancillary Justice by Ann Leckie**

On a remote, icy planet, the soldier known as Breq is drawing closer to completing her quest.

Once, she was the Justice of Toren - a colossal starship with an artificial intelligence linking thousands of soldiers in the service of the Radch, the empire that conquered the galaxy.

Now, an act of treachery has ripped it all away, leaving her with one fragile human body, unanswered questions, and a burning desire for vengeance.

events.title: Book Discussion: A Memory Called Empire by Arkady Martine
events.description:
**A Memory Called Empire by Arkady Martine**

Ambassador Mahit Dzmare arrives in the center of the multi-system Teixcalaanli Empire only to discover that her predecessor, the previous ambassador from their small but fiercely independent mining Station, has died. But no one will admit that his death wasn't an accident―or that Mahit might be next to die, during a time of political instability in the highest echelons of the imperial court.

Now, Mahit must discover who is behind the murder, resc...

events.title: Book Discussion: Dreadful by Caitlin Rozakis
events.description:
**Dreadful by Caitlin Rozakis**

**A sharp-witted, high fantasy farce featuring killer moat squid, toxic masculinity, evil wizards and a garlic festival - all at once. Perfect for fans of T. Kingfisher, K. J. Parker and Travis Baldree.**

It’s bad enough waking up in a half-destroyed evil wizard’s workshop with no eyebrows, no memories, and no idea how long you have before the Dread Lord Whomever shows up to murder you horribly and then turn your skull into a goblet or something.

It’s a lot ...

events.title: Book Discussion: The Master and Margarita by Mikhail Bulgakov
events.description:
**The Master and Margarita by Mikhail Bulgakov**

An audacious revision of the stories of Faust and Pontius Pilate, *The Master and Margarita* is recognized as one of the essential classics of modern Russian literature. The novel's vision of Soviet life in the 1930s is so ferociously accurate that it could not be published during its author's lifetime and appeared only in a censored edition in the 1960s. Its truths are so enduring that its language has become part of the common Russian speech...

events.title: Coffee, Art, and Live Music
events.description:
Please RSVP if you plan on attending so I know how many tables to grab. 🙃

It's a “pay-what-you-can” show, as they say, so if you can afford a few bucks, it will be a great encouragement for the musicians!

Your tips will help keep the live music coming to Cargo Coffee. 🙂

Come hang out, drink some coffee (or your preferred beverage), and MAKE SOME ART! 😀

Or just come hang out.

No requirements, but I will bring extra supplies for watercolor and drawing if anyone wants to try either.

I'll b...

events.title: Let’s Go Celebrate MSO!
events.description:
Madison Symphony Orchestra (MSO) is celebrating 100 years this year! Overture Center is hosting a free weekend with food vendors, music performances, art and music events all weekend long.

I’ll be there on Saturday with a friend at 8:30am until 5:30pm when the organ concert ends. The event is also happening on Sunday but I won’t be there, but feel free to attend it afterwards!

They have a few concerts you can RSVP for as well:
* [Family Festival Concert](https://madisonsymphony.org/event/fe...

events.title: Let's go Rock Climbing at Greater Heights!
events.description:
Let's go rock climbing!

I used to go several times a week, but haven't been very consistent the past few years, but I'm starting again.

Here's a link to their waiver:
[https://www.ghclimbing.com/visitor-agreement](https://www.ghclimbing.com/visitor-agreement)

We'll meet at 6:00 pm and climb until we're tired or 8:00pm, whichever comes first 🙃

This gym has rope climbing and bouldering, so it'll be a good time

If you have any questions or have never been rock climbing before, feel free to ...

events.title: Trivia @ Schwoegler's Park Towne Lanes
events.description:
Join Team Random Facts for our usually weekly west side Monday night trivia at **Schwoegler's Park Towne Lanes**. (Occasional Mondays may be at other locations.)

Note the 7:00 start time, but try to arrive a bit early.

https://schwoegler.com/bar-and-grill/

events.title: Trivia Meetup @ Me&Julio in Fitchburg
events.description:
Do you enjoy eating delicious TexMex cuisine while a stranger asks you random questions? Great! Come on in and join us for trivia night at Me&Julio!

There's more than just Mexican food too. With burgers, chicken, steak & fish, soups & salads, vegetarian options, appetizers & desserts there's bound to be something for everyone.

Menu: https://www.meandjuliorestaurant.com/menu-fitchburg

Not sure you can contribute in a game of trivia? Consider this. If you are able to answer even one question...

events.title: Board Games at the Memorial Union
events.description: I'd like to play some board games on a Monday but don't see a regular event for this elsewhere and so would like to plan one. I'm planning on bringing: Dominion, Great Western Trail, Project L, and Flip 7. Others are welcome to bring games as well. Please let me know if you have any questions. Food is close by on the terrace and we could potentially play on the terrace if conditions are nice and not too windy.

events.description:
**Where:** Madison Central Library, Room 209
**When:** Every Saturday, 10:00 AM

Join us for an hour of writing! We’ve discovered that it’s strikingly helpful to write with other writers. See if it’s true for you. events.title: Movie Club: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs (1937)
events.description:
With pick #34 on the Top 100, we travel back to 1937 and to something that had genuinely never existed before in America: a feature-length animated film. Walt Disney's gamble was so widely doubted that Hollywood called it "Disney's Folly."

Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs is the story we all think we know: a princess, a wicked queen, a poisoned apple, a glass coffin. But people who've revisited it are often surprised by how strange and dark it still feels — how genuinely menacing the Queen is...

events.title: Book Club (FTP Edition): Parable of the Talents, Octavia Butler
events.description:
We first met Lauren Olamina in *Parable of the Sower*, and her vision and determination stayed with me for weeks. *Parable of the Talents* picks up right where we left off, and somehow Octavia Butler makes the stakes even higher.

Lauren has made it out of the chaos of *Sower* with a small community, a shared belief system she calls Earthseed, and a fragile sense of hope.

The America of *Talents* has found its strongman — a politician running on "Make America Great Again" (!) whose followers...

events.title: Album Club: Dr. Dre, The Chronic (1992)
events.description:
Coming in at #37 on the Top 100 is Dr. Dre's The Chronic, the album that gave hip-hop a whole new sound and made the West Coast the center of the rap universe almost overnight.

Released in 1992, this was Dre's first solo record after leaving the legendary group N.W.A, and he used it to invent something called G-funk: slow, rolling grooves built on deep bass, whining synthesizers, and samples of 1970s funk (think Parliament-Funkadelic).

It's also the album that introduced the world to a then...
